subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – Dust of Snow & Fire and Ice
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main events and themes of both poems
Identify simple symbols and their meanings
Develop basic comprehension and summarization skills
Recognize the poet’s message about nature and human emotions
Encourage creative expression through drawing and writing

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poems and Poet
topics_covered: Overview of 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'
teaching_strategy: Introduce Robert Frost and his style in simple language
Ask students to share any experiences with nature or emotions
student_activities: Discuss the titles and predict what each poem might be about
Share personal experiences of small incidents affecting mood
assessment: Ask students to name the poet and poems

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – Dust of Snow
topics_covered: Understand the main event and emotional change
teaching_strategy: Read line by line and explain simply
Encourage students to visualize the scene
assessment: Ask students how the poet’s mood changed

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – Fire and Ice
topics_covered: Discuss the simple meanings of fire (desire) and ice (hate)
teaching_strategy: Read and explain line by line
Give simple examples of desire and hate
student_activities: Identify words representing fire and ice
assessment: Oral Q&A on symbols and theme

lesson_plan.title: Creative Reflection and Discussion
topics_covered: Recognizing small events or emotions in daily life
teaching_strategy: Encourage students to connect poems to their own experiences
Summarize key points and symbols on a chart
student_activities: Write a short paragraph about a small event or emotion that changed their mood
Draw a simple illustration of crow & snow or fire & ice
assessment: Class discussion on lessons learned from the poems

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of both poems
Images of crow, hemlock, fire, and ice
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and interactive. Highlight how nature or emotions can affect mood. Encourage students to relate poems to personal experiences for better understanding.
